data.description:
Distinct from eaa9 (supersedes/duplicates awareness). This gap: the completion-verification close gate requires EVERY acceptance criterion to be met as literally written, with the only escapes being --force-close (a bypass) or the idea->closed hack. It has NO legitimate author-driven path for two real cases:

1. ACs INVALIDATED BY THE FIX. When the correct fix proves the reported premise false (a false-positive reclassification), the original ACs become unsatisfiable-by-correct-design, yet the verifier still demands they be met. There is no way to record 'this criterion is moot because the fix reclassified the premise, here is the evidence' — and because the criteria are codebase-verifiable, an attestation COMMENT is explicitly rejected.

2. BOT-AUTHORED ALERT tickets carry AUTO-GENERATED ACs (triage templates) that assume the alert condition is real and actionable. When it isn't (false positive) or when closure is owned by an automation (the canary force-closes bot alerts by design), the human/author completion path can never pass.

REPRO: bug f436-fac8-382f-47ec — a rebar-bridge[bot] binding-drift alert. Its ACs ('each reported drift is triaged', 'bridge-fsck reports empty binding_drift') assume the 163 dangling entries were real. The merged fix (Gerrit 848) proved them FALSE POSITIVES (alive aged-out bindings); live bridge-fsck shows 0 actionable drift. The verifier still FAILs the close ('2 unmet criteria') and rejects the live-evidence comment as insufficient for codebase-verifiable criteria. The only closes available are the canary's own --force-close or a manual force-close.

EXPECTED: a legitimate, audited author path to close when ACs are demonstrably moot/invalidated (with cited evidence the verifier accepts), and/or a first-class notion of bot-authored alert tickets whose closure is owned by their filing automation and not subject to author-completion verification. Same 'lifecycle requirement not propagated to a legitimate branch' family as eaa9 / ed13 / 0e15.

Discovered during the rebar-debug batch while trying to close f436 after its fix landed.
data.title: Completion verifier has no legitimate close path when acceptance criteria are invalidated by the fix or are auto-generated/unverifiable
